channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: How to create a portfolio career playbook for long-term consulting stability
item.description: During a recent digital event, social impact career coach Kelsi Kriitmaa outlined a practical framework for moving from ad hoc consulting to a structured portfolio career.

item.title: Young Afghan Taekwondo Women Coach Chose Resistance over Surrender to Taliban
item.description: The author is an Afghanistan-based female journalist, trained with Finnish support before the Taliban take-over. Her identity is withheld for security reasons.

item.title: Mercy Corps to become Prosper Global
item.description: The rebrand comes amid unprecedented funding shocks and aims to better convey the values that the humanitarian nonprofit espouses.

item.title: A UN Committee Election Could Worsen Civil Society Access to the World Body
item.description: Far from headlines, the United Nations Committee on Non-Governmental Organizations has the power of picking which civil society voices get heard at the organization. The election of the committee’s newest 19 members is slated to occur on April 8. As a subsidiary body of the UN Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC), the committee decides which ... Read more

item.title: Devex Dish: How the Iran war could trigger a global food crisis
item.description: Plus, the latest from the Asian Development Bank’s food systems forum.

item.title: What happens when AI labs and social enterprises build together?
item.description: Social enterprises are working with AI labs to test, adapt, and improve language models in local contexts. This episode of Devex's Global Progress in the AI Era podcast explores whether these partnerships could reinforce existing inequalities — or help address them.

item.title: ADB gets moving on its $40B commitment to food systems
item.description: At the Asia and the Pacific Food Systems Forum, conversations centered around how ADB should spend the remainder of a splashy $40 billion commitment to transforming food systems that it made last year.

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 18 March: Lebanon displacement tops one million as nuclear safety concerns raised
item.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with ongoing strikes across the region and rising humanitarian needs. The UN nuclear watchdog reports that a projectile struck the premises of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant, with no damage or injuries. In Lebanon, the humanitarian situation is worsening, with hundreds reported killed and more than one million people displaced amid continued airstrikes and extensive displacement orders. UN agencies are scaling up emergency assistance despite mounting challenges. Stay with us for live updates from across the United Nations system. App users can follow coverage here.

item.title: Middle East war risks undermining Syria’s fragile recovery, Security Council hears
item.description: The international community must step up efforts to support Syria amid the current conflict raging in the Middle East, two senior UN officials told the Security Council on Wednesday.

item.title: World News in Brief: Yemen appeal, Middle East war roils Somalia, needs grow in Colombia
item.description: UN aid teams in Yemen on Wednesday warned that the situation in the war-torn nation remains dire in a call for $2 billion in aid to deliver lifesaving assistance for 12 million people.

item.title: Humanitarian needs in Gaza deepen as aid access remains constrained
item.description: Humanitarian needs are continuing to grow again across Gaza, the UN agency assisting Palestine refugees (UNRWA) said on Wednesday, amid mounting pressures on aid delivery and the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: UN mourns constitutional lawyer-turned top crisis diplomat, Nicholas Haysom
item.description: The UN is mourning the loss of Nicholas Haysom, the Secretary-General’s Special Representative for South Sudan, whose went from advising Nelson Mandela following the historic anti-apartheid struggle to becoming a “tireless” champion of crisis diplomacy for the UN in Africa, Asia and the Middle East.

item.title: Devex Newswire: UN cuts deepen debate over reform and relevance
item.description: Experts warn that budget cuts at the U.N. risk shrinking the global body without addressing its fundamental flaws. Plus, the U.S. pushes language excluding transgender women, and USADF prevails in a case against the Trump administration.

item.title: Africa’s Minerals Boon, Cautious Optimism Amid Geopolitical Disruptions
item.description: Africa’s eye on minerals as the be-all-and-cure-all for the continent’s development agenda is being tested by geopolitical gamesmanship as global superpowers jostle to carve new spheres of influence. Amid the turmoil unleashed by the United States President Donald Trump administration’s trade tariffs, African minerals have faced price volatility, which analysts say points to the fragility […]

item.title: Watchdog: US State Department not answering aid oversight questions
item.description: The Government Accountability Office is "waiting for up to eight months for basic answers to questions," its director told members of Congress.

item.title: CHINA: ‘The State Is Using Generative AI to Engineer Reality Through Informational Gaslighting’
item.description: CIVICUS discusses China’s tech-enabled repression with Fergus Ryan, a Senior Analyst at the Australian Strategic Policy Institute (ASPI), where he specialises in how the Chinese Communist Party shapes global information environments through censorship, propaganda and platform governance. His research includes a major study on China’s AI ecosystem and its human rights impacts, as well […]

item.title: Beyond Stereotypes: Reclaiming Muslim Histories during Ramadan
item.description: In public discourse today, Muslims often appear as subjects of debate rather than authors of their own histories. Discussions about Muslim societies tend to revolve around geopolitics, security or conflict, leaving little space for the cultural, artistic and intellectual traditions that have shaped Muslim communities across centuries. Reclaiming these narratives is therefore about reclaiming narrative […]

item.title: A Remotely-Piloted Weapon That Targets Civilians in War Zones
item.description: As the world continues to be weighed down in political and military turmoil, drones are being increasingly used as weapons of war in a rash of ongoing conflicts—including Ukraine vs Russia, Israel vs Palestine, US vs Iran and Israel vs Lebanon, plus in civil wars in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Sudan and […]
